title: AI Ethics in Business: Beyond the Buzzwords
author: AI Systems Engineering Team
excerpt: Responsible AI isn't just a PR exercise. It's risk management, competitive advantage, and in many jurisdictions, the law.

content.text: In 2018, Amazon scrapped an AI recruiting tool because it discriminated against women. In 2020, Clearview AI faced lawsuits for scraping billions of photos without consent. These aren't edge cases — they're warnings of what happens when ethics is an afterthought.
Ethical AI isn't charity. It's business-critical:

items: EU AI Act fines up to 6% of global revenue for non-compliance
Biased models create legal liability and reputational damage
Explainable models win enterprise contracts over black boxes
Transparency builds trust with customers and regulators
Audit training data for demographic representation
Test model performance across different groups
Build human-in-the-loop systems for high-stakes decisions
Establish an AI ethics review board

content.text: Companies that build ethics into their AI systems from day one have an advantage. Their models are more robust, their processes are audit-ready, and their customers trust them more. In a world where AI regulation is accelerating, proactive ethics is a moat.
